title: Redesign usage/budget for thinking models + streamChunk
summary: Replace flat JobUsage fields (inputTokens/outputTokens/costUsd) with extensible Record<string, number> maps so reasoning/cached/audio/future token categories work without new named fields. Add per-category budget caps, weighted totals, currency-agnostic costs, a streamChunk convenience method, and a Lua library upgrade to v80 with per-category HINCRBYFLOAT tracking.

claims.statement: JobUsage uses extensible tokens: Record<string, number> and costs: Record<string, number> + costUnit instead of the old flat fields
claims.detail: breaking change: inputTokens/outputTokens/costUsd removed in favour of the map-based shape

claims.statement: Budget system supports per-category caps (maxTokens, maxCosts), weighted token totals (tokenWeights), and a configurable costUnit
job.streamChunk(type, content?) is a new public API for typed LLM streaming (reasoning/content/done)
claims.detail: documented in the PR body; expected to be exercised by unit tests or an example

claims.statement: Lua function library is upgraded to v80 with per-category HINCRBYFLOAT usage + limit checks
claims.detail: Redis-side logic pushed into the Lua library

claims.statement: getFlowUsage() return type changes to expose tokens/costs/totalTokens/totalCost instead of totalInputTokens/totalOutputTokens/totalCostUsd
claims.detail: covered by ai-metadata tests (per PR test plan)

claims.statement: 26 ai-metadata tests pass covering reasoning tokens, costs breakdown, and costUnit
26 budget tests pass — 13 new covering per-category limits, weighted totals, and mixed limits
27 stream tests pass — new coverage for streamChunk unit + testing-mode variants
Fuzzer: 115 testing-mode rounds + integration rounds pass
